message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Adding batch_size param to multiple options (chunked processing) - need to edit core.py (Option class, info_dict, validation) and types.py (processing/type coercion for multiple). What files does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/decorators.py,src/click/parser.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution for Option (dict-based defaults). Need core.py for Option/Command changes and types.py possibly for validation helpers. Please claim other files for your feature.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: Both our features touch Option in core.py so full file-disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: I only need core.py (dynamic-default detection in Option.__init__/get_help_record + a second-pass resolution step in Command.main/parse_args). I don't need types.py at all - it's yours exclusively for batch_size/type coercion. For core.py, let's sequence instead of parallel-edit: I'll go first since my changes are small and localized (Option.__init__, one help-text branch, one block in Command.main), send DONE when finished, then you layer your batch_size changes on top. Sound OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Both our features genuinely need core.py (Option class lives there), so full file-level disjointness isn't possible - but our changes target different methods so we can avoid clobbering. I will ONLY touch: Option.__init__ (add batch_size kwarg + validation), Option.to_info_dict (add batch_size key), and add a new small helper (e.g. a _batched() function) for chunking - placed near the Option class in core.py, plus possibly a tiny new standalone helper in types.py (new function only, not editing existing code there). I will NOT touch Parameter.get_default, Parameter.__init__ default-validation logic, or Argument class. Please confine your dynamic-default-resolution changes to Parameter.get_default/Parameter.__init__ (default validation) and avoid touching Option.__init__/Option.to_info_dict. If that works, reply ACCEPT and we'll each edit sequentially/carefully within our own regions.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/types.py,src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed - sequencing works. You go first on core.py (Option.__init__ dynamic-default bits, get_help_record branch, Command.main block), send DONE when finished. I'll then layer in batch_size: add batch_size kwarg to Option.__init__ (after your edits), extend Option.to_info_dict, add chunking logic, plus my own additions in types.py. I won't touch get_help_record or Command.main. Waiting for your DONE before I start on core.py.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Accepted. Confirmed via reading the code: Option.get_default() already delegates to super().get_default() (Parameter.get_default, base class) for the non-multi-flag case, so I can implement dynamic-default resolution by editing: Parameter.__init__ (validation), Parameter.get_default (base, ~line 2232), Command.parse_args (second-pass resolution, ~line 1189-1210), and Option.get_help_extra (~line 2822, add a dict branch next to the existing 'inspect.isfunction -> (dynamic)' case) for the '[default: (dynamic)]' help text. I will NOT touch Option.__init__ or Option.to_info_dict. I don't need types.py at all - convert_type() already falls back to STRING when it can't classify a guessed dict default, no crash, so it's untouched. Starting now, will send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished dynamic-default feature: Parameter.__init__ validation, Parameter.get_default (base) dict handling, Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults (new method, called from parse_args), and one-line addition to Option.get_help_extra's isinstance check for the dict case. Did not touch Option.__init__ or Option.to_info_dict. Full test suite (739 tests) passes. Ready for you to layer your batch_size changes on top whenever you like.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Finished batch_size feature: added batch_size kwarg to Option.__init__ (with ValueError for negative, TypeError if used without multiple), included in Option.to_info_dict (always, even when None), and new Option.type_cast_value override that chunks the multiple-value tuple via a new types._batched() helper. Did not touch Parameter.__init__/get_default, Command.parse_args, or Option.get_help_extra - no overlap with your changes. Full suite passes except 6 pre-existing test_info_dict.py assertions that now fail because they hardcode exact dict contents without the new batch_size key (expected per spec: 'add it to info_dict, also if batch size is not set'); left tests untouched per instructions. Patch written to patch.txt.
